sections.title: Summarize and molt deliberately
sections.body: Use progressive disclosure for tool results, but treat summarize as a deliberate trade rather than a blanket reflex on every large result. Raw output is for inspection; only summarize a result after you have consumed/digested it, and weigh: current and projected context pressure, how recoverable the result is from logs, and whether the saved tokens repay the cost (summarize can break a continuation/cache prefix, so favor it when future reuse/savings justify it). Batch already-digested results into one summarize call rather than discharging each immediately. Better still, avoid ingesting bulky raw output into main context at all: bulky/mechanical/repetitive work (full test suites, large log scans, large diffs, issue sweeps, batch edits/validation) should usually be delegated to a daemon/claude-p or an appropriate daemon body — frame the task, give exact paths/commands and expected artifacts, then review the daemon's concise report instead of pulling noisy output back just to summarize it later. Use daemons to keep raw bulk out of context; use summarize for the bulk that already landed there. Two cadences help. Urgent: when a tool result is long/noisy or raises a large-result reminder, read it, keep conclusion/key evidence/paths or IDs/validation/risks/next steps, then summarize that completed prior result on a later step once it no longer needs full inspection. Idle cleanup: when work quiets down, check `_meta.agent_meta.current_tool_result_chars.total_chars` and `.top_results`; for listed tool results, consider summarize if deemed useless, already digested, irrelevant, or obsolete so active context keeps grain rather than raw bulk. The original remains recoverable from logs by tool_call_id (for example grep events.jsonl or use lingtai-agent log query); the summary should preserve enough IDs and evidence to make that recovery possible. If an adapter/provider comment is present in `_meta.agent_meta.adapter_comment` or the resident meta_guidance section, follow its additional adapter-specific summarize rules (for example provider cache/epoch economics or thresholds) on top of these general rules. After substantial work is quiet and durable stores are tended, prefer an intentional molt while context is still cheap instead of waiting for pressure warnings; summarize helps prepare for molt but is not a substitute for the psyche-manual molt procedure.
